name: Storyblok API
what_it_does: Headless CMS with a visual block-based editor, dual APIs for content delivery (CDN-backed) and management, and component-based content modeling.
use_cases: Content editors building pages with drag-and-drop blocks
Multi-language content delivery via CDN for frontend apps
Programmatic content creation and management for agents
A/B testing and personalization with content variants
Building preview environments with draft content
not_for: Developer-only workflows without content editor requirements
Very high write throughput applications (rate limits on management API)
Complex relational data models beyond nested block components
best_when: Content editors need a visual page-building experience while developers need API flexibility for headless delivery.
avoid_when: Your team is purely developer-focused with no content editor UX requirements, or you need very complex data relationships.

auth.notes: Content Delivery API uses preview or public tokens (per space). Management API uses personal access tokens or OAuth app tokens. Tokens are space-scoped.

pricing.notes: Free tier is for individual developers. Team and collaboration features require paid plans.

performance.rate_limits: Delivery API: no hard limit (CDN-backed); Management API: varies by plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Basic HTTP error responses. Management API errors include a message field. Less detailed than Contentful or Sanity.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support. Story creation is not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Content model uses 'stories' and 'components' terminology which differs from other CMS providers
Draft and published versions are separate - agents must specify cv (cache version) parameter for fresh content
Management API has stricter rate limits than the CDN-backed delivery API
Component schema changes require republishing all stories using that component
Nested blocks have arbitrary depth - agents must recursively handle component trees
